mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts above 1.1 million signals a strengthening demand for housing, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1100000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% signals a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 5.5 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 112 signals a strengthening supply chain, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 112 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 11700 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 11700 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 2.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 2.5 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 138000 signals a strengthening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 138000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 384631 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 384631 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
